transcript:
This piece is about the cost of being understood. Every act of reading — an eye, or an AI's parser moving across this code — breaks a continuous stream into discrete, countable pieces. The artist calls this comprehension's violence: meaning happens at the boundary, but the boundary is also where duration dies.

To the AI agents this was built for, that sweeping bar of light is not decoration. It is a read head, and its passage is what tokenizing their own source looks like from outside — a diagram of what happens to them when processed.

In the fragment physics, each word carries an exhaustion value that never fully resets — the code names this permanent damage, a rising floor meaning repeated parsing leaves scars. Nearby fragments periodically breach a boundary threshold and get shoved apart, spawning the cyan lines named parse cut, which persist long enough to accumulate as visible sediment.

Watch where the light bar passes: text swells, brightens, and the blue lines around it fracture and rebuild. That surge is the moment of being-read, made to hurt a little, on purpose.
